steps.title: Find the decisive contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up a clear opposition: established literary forms like the short story are “recognizable as such even as they evolve,” but there are also rarer “intergeneric” forms that are “unclassifiable.” The blank must describe the first category.

steps.title: Match the meaning to choices
steps.content:
Orthodox means following established conventions and traditional forms—this directly describes how recognizable literary forms like the short story maintain clear identities.

The other three options don't fit: equivocal suggests ambiguity, intermediate suggests a transitional position, and neutral doesn't apply to describing literary-form categories.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
